name: Mafdet Extended
description: Mafdet, often depicted as a feline or ichneumon (mongoose-like animal), was an important goddess in ancient Egyptian religion, particularly during the First Dynasty.  She represented justice, protection against venomous creatures, and swift punishment of wrongdoing.  Her role extended beyond simply warding off snakes and scorpions; she embodied the swiftness and impartiality of divine justice, acting as a protector of sacred spaces and individuals.  Unlike some deities associated with specific locations, Mafdet's influence was more generalized, providing a broader sense of security and divine retribution. She was seen as a guardian figure, ensuring order and safety from the dangers lurking in the shadows and the threats posed by enemies. Her association with felines highlights their agility and hunting prowess, further emphasizing her role as a swift and effective punisher. She was often depicted with a headdress of knives, symbolizing her sharp and decisive nature in delivering justice. Mafdet's importance declined somewhat as other deities gained prominence, but her role as a protector against venom and a symbol of swift justice remained a part of the Egyptian religious landscape.
subtitle: Swift Justice and Venomous Protection
appearance: Mafdet is often depicted as a lithe and agile feline, resembling a cheetah or wildcat. She can also appear as an ichneumon, a mongoose-like creature known for its ability to kill snakes. Regardless of her form, she is typically adorned with a headdress of knives, symbolizing her swift and decisive nature in delivering justice.  Her eyes are sharp and piercing, reflecting her keen senses and unwavering focus on protecting the innocent.  Her fur is often depicted as dappled or striped, providing camouflage in the shadows. Sometimes she is depicted with human female body and the head of a feline.
habitat: Mafdet's presence is felt in both the physical and spiritual realms. She protects sacred spaces, such as temples and tombs, as well as the homes of those who invoke her protection. She is also believed to inhabit the desert fringes, where venomous creatures thrive, and the shadows where injustice lurks.
behavior: Mafdet is a fierce protector, acting swiftly and decisively against those who threaten the innocent. She is known for her impartiality and unwavering commitment to justice. While she is a fearsome punisher of the wicked, she is also a compassionate guardian of those who seek her protection. She is often invoked to ward off venomous creatures and to ensure safety from harm.

keyMyths.title: Mafdet and the Pharaoh
keyMyths.description: Mafdet protected the Pharaoh from snakes and scorpions, ensuring his safety and well-being. Her swiftness in neutralizing venom saved his life on multiple occasions, solidifying her role as a protector of the royal lineage.

sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
sources.work: Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
sources.description: Provides a comprehensive overview of Mafdet's role and attributes.
sources.text: Provides a comprehensive overview of Mafdet's role and attributes.

sources.author: Manly P. Hall
sources.work: The Secret Teachings of All Ages
sources.description: Offers insights into the symbolic meaning of Mafdet's association with justice and protection.
